details.text: Derivative: تَبَارَكَ – “To be blessed”. The فِعْل الماضِي (past tense) can be used as a supplication, like in the statement: “May he be blessed” or as a statement of fact, like inn the statement: “He is blessed in of Himself”. بَرَكَة (Barakah - Blessing) means an increase in goodness beyond expectation. تَبَارَكَ refers to having the power to bring goodness out of things like no one else.

Derivative: سُحْقًا is like بُعْدًا which means: “Away with (him or them)” meaning take them away from here or be gone with them. سُحْقًا is stronger than بُعْدًا and implies to take them far away and into the depths (of hellfire).
- “In the unseen” can mean either 1) In private i.e., (they fear Allah) when nobody can see them, or 2) The unseen relates to the Rabb i.e., (they fear Him) although they cannot see Him.
Derivative: اللَّطِيفُ is the Most Subtle, who knows the intricacies and delicacies of matters i.e., He knows them inside out.

Derivative: ذَلُولًا means to be tamed and is used in origin with animals which have been made docile where humans can interact with them easily. Here it is being used in relation to the earth الْأَرْضَ (Al-Ard) and it means that it has been pliant and accommodating for us.
Derivative: تَمُورُ from مَارَ means to shake and convulse, or to move and sway quickly back and forth or side to side. Here, it means in a way that leads to your destruction.
Derivative: حَاصِبًا is a strong or violent wind that carries with it pebbles, stones, dirt, sand etc.
Derivative: نَكِيرِ “My Nakeer” means “my condemnation” or “the hardship or difficulty that I brought upon them”.
Derivative: يَقْبِضْنَ means to clasp or close the grip of the hand. Here it refers to the birds closing and drawing their wings into their body as opposed to them being spread.

Derivative: صَافَّاتٍ is from صَفٌّ which means a row. صَافٌّ (Saaff) is the اِسْمُ الفاعِل (Ism ul-Fa’il – Doer) form of it. It is used to indicate to straightness or lining something up in a straight manner. Birds are called صَافَّاتٍ (Saaffaat) when they spread their wings straight.

- Here the actions of the bird are described with and اِسْمُ الفاعِل (Ism ul-Fa’il – Doer) followed by a فِعْل (verb) in the present tense. The اِسْمُ الفاعِل (Ism ul-Fa’il – Doer) is used to indicate greater permanency in the actions whilst the فِعْل (verb) in the present tense is used more for an action which is not permanent. It is observable that when a bird flies, its wings are outstretched the majority of the time and that is interrupted from time to time by them pulling them in.
Derivative: عُتُوٍّ disobedience based upon arrogance.
Derivative: نُفُورٍ means aversion i.e., a rejection based upon hate and distaste of something.
Derivative: لَجُّوا from لّجَّ – يَلَجُّ – لَجَاجَةٌ. It means to go deep into something with persistency, to keep doing something, to be stubborn and persistent upon it.

Derivative: مُكِبًّا is from كَبَّ which means to flip something over onto its opposite side or to turn it upside down. مُكِبًّا is from أَكَبَّ which means that he has been made to turned upside down where he walks upside down upon his face. Either he makes himself do that or someone else. It is used to provide the image of someone that does not know the reality of what exists around him as his face is stuck to the ground, someone who is aimless and lost and whose focus is only attached to the worldly and material things.
Derivative: أَنْشَأَ means to build up, to make something rise, bring into being, originate and create from nothing.

Derivative: ذَرَأَ means to create something without there being a like of it already in existence i.e., a model to copy or imitate. The other meaning for ذَرَأَ is to spread or scatter out and make numerous.
Derivative: تَدَّعُونَ can mean to make claims i.e., this is what you used to make (false) claims about. Or it can mean to make a call for or to invite upon yourselves.
Derivative: زُلْفَةً means to come or approach closer and closer.
Derivative: يُجِيرُ means to grant protection. In origin related to protecting someone and giving them refuge as your neighbour (جَارٌ).

- In the statement آمَنَّا بِهِ (We have believed in Him) the بِهِ (in Him) follows آمَنَّا (We have believed), whilst in His statement عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْنَا (Upon Him we have relied) the عَلَيْهِ (on Him) precedes تَوَكَّلْنَا (We have relied). By putting the عَلَيْهِ (on Him) first it is indicative of اِخْتِصاص (Ikhtisaas) which means exclusivity, which implies that we rely on Allah exclusively and nobody else. In the statement آمَنَّا بِهِ (We have believed in Him) it is not an exclusive form as we believe in Allah, but also those other matters which Allah has commanded us to believe in like the Day of Judgement, His Angels, Prophets and Books.
Derivative: فُطُورٍ refers to any crack, break or tear that goes down vertically.
Derivative: تَفَاوُتٍ refers to a difference in qualities between things i.e., a disparity or inconsistency.
Derivative: غَوْرًا means to be sunk into the earth.
Derivative: حَسِيرٌ from حَسِرَ which means to be fatigued, exhausted and pained.

Derivative: خَاسِئًا comes from خَسَأَ which means to shoo, drive or chase something away. خَاسِئًا is something that has been shooed away and as such has been spurned. It also means to be exhausted and worn out, or humbled.
Derivative: الدُّنْيَا meaning the lowest or nearest. The feminine of أَدْنَى (Closest – nearest) from the verb دَنَا which means to be close.
Derivative: تَفُورُ from فَارَ which means to boil over violently with steam and bubbles bursting forth.
Derivative: شَهِيقًا is the sound of the donkey braying and here it refers to a terrible horrendous sound.
Derivative: تَمَيَّزُ – تَمَيَّزَ means to separate.
- تَمَيَّزُ مِنَ الْغَيْظِ literally means: “To separate or break up due to anger” and it means: “To burst or erupt in anger or rage”.
